response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant AI-native experience — current Staff Software Engineer at Google working on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure — plus real high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of requests per second) and strong TypeScript across Meta and RIDE. However, Python, a must-have, appears nowhere in the resume or skills list (the systems work was in DLang), and the resume shows breadth as a hands-on polyglot craftsman more than evidence of directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or operational ownership at staff scope. Worth a screen to probe Python depth and strategic scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at Google — directly matches the AI-native engineering baseline
Proven high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js track record with solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python evidence anywhere in the resume — a stated must-have for the role
Little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or on-call/operational ownership
High-throughput systems experience is several years old and in DLang, a niche stack that may not transfer directly

key_factors.factor: AI-native / agentic tooling experience at Google
